segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a blurred forest scene featuring a green circular logo with a white 'B' and the text 'THE BOX ON BETA SQUAD', as a deep, authoritative male narrator announces, "Today on Beta Squad, the boys will be surviving 24 hours in the Australian jungle, the outback." This is followed by a breathtaking aerial view of a mist-covered Australian jungle with mountains, accompanied by the text '24 HOURS IN THE AUSTRALIAN JUNGLE', while the narrator continues, "Home to the most poisonous spiders, snakes, kangaroos, and most importantly, AJ's family." The scene shifts to a close-up of a crocodile’s face with the text 'HOME TO THE MOST POISONOUS SPIDERS', then kangaroos grazing with the text 'THE BONSAI SPORTS DREAMS'. Two men in beige outdoor gear, one wearing a black beanie and the other a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat, are shown looking at a phone, with the text 'BOTH TEAMS SPLIT INTO 2 TEAMS', as the narrator explains, "The boys are split into two teams." A man in a black jacket and cap, identified as 'AJ SHAHEEL THE GAMES MASTER', holds a megaphone, with the text 'SEEING THE TASK MASTER', and the narrator states, "Both teams will have to complete challenges set by the taskmaster AJ to avoid having to spend a night in the outback." An aerial shot shows a group of people on a rocky outcrop with the text 'TO AVOID HAVING TO SPEND A NIGHT IN THE OUTBACK'. The title 'BETA SQUAD 24 HOURS IN THE JUNGLE' appears in bold, colorful letters, followed by the narrator's call to "Let's meet the teams." Team Beta is introduced with two men, Kenny and Sharky, sitting on a log, smiling and gesturing confidently, with the text 'WE'VE GOT THE STRENGTH' and 'THAT'S WHAT WE DO NEED TO WIN'. Kenny, speaking with a clear, confident voice, declares, "Alright, it's team Beta, Kenny and Sharky," and then, with Sharky's interjection, asserts, "We got the knowledge, we got the strength, the integrity... that's what we do need to win. We got this." Sharky humorously adds, "We're screwed," with a laugh. Next, 'TEAM WINNER-CHAMPIONS' is introduced with Chunkz and Niko, also seated on a log, with Niko wearing a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat, and the text 'AND WE'RE HERE TO WIN FOR VIBES' and 'YES, I'M HERE FOR A GOOD TIME'. Chunkz, speaking confidently, announces, "Alright guys, we're team Winner Champions, and we're here to bring the good vibes," while Niko energetically adds, "Yes, I'm here for a good time, not a long time." Chunkz further expresses confidence, "I feel like me and Chunkz together, we'll bring it home," to which Niko exclaims, "Yes!" The final shot is an aerial view of a rugged landscape with two framed boxes—one green, one yellow—each containing a team, with the text 'THE BOX SHELL BE HAPPY FOR LONG' and 'IN TWO SEPARATE LOCATIONS', indicating the teams are split and competing in different areas, as the narrator ominously states, "I don't think they'll be happy for long. The boys will be dropped off..." all set against a backdrop of suspenseful background music.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a sweeping aerial view of the rugged, forested Australian Outback, establishing a remote setting 15,236km from home, as a narrator explains that the participants are in two separate locations for their first challenge. Inside a moving dark-colored SUV, Kenny and Sharky, both in beige outdoor gear and black beanies, react with growing concern. Kenny's voice expresses anxiety, asking "Where are we going, man?" and noting "no service, nothing," while Sharky adds to the worry about needing help. Distorted, drawn-out vocal sounds punctuate their distress. As they navigate a dusty, tree-lined dirt road, Kenny voices his genuine concern, stating, "I'm genuinely, I'm actually concerned," and questions why "AJ" brought them to this place with "the steepest hills God's created," calling him a "short little prick." The narrator confirms that Kenny and Sharky are the first to arrive at their destination, where they will have "little to no supplies to survive." The vehicle stops, and Kenny steps out, visibly stunned, exclaiming "What the f-" and "Oh!" Sharky echoes his shock with "Oh my word!" as the camera reveals vast, imposing cliffs and dense forest. Despite the isolation, Kenny remarks, "That is beautiful though," but Sharky quickly adds a pragmatic note: "It's beautiful, but not when we have to like survive out here." Kenny then shouts "No!" in increasing panic, while Sharky observes Kenny's fatigue. Kenny confirms, "I'm already tired." The segment concludes with Kenny and Sharky, backpacks on, walking away from the vehicle into the wilderness, reaching their destination.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Team Beta, Kenny and Sharky, stepping out of a dark SUV into the Australian Outback, both dressed in beige outdoor gear with backpacks, Kenny in a black beanie and Sharky in a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat. A narrator's voice explains their car had trouble reaching its destination. Kenny confirms, stating their car 'has not been able to make it up the hill,' forcing them to walk the rest of the journey. As they hike through a dry, wooded landscape along a dirt path, Sharky expresses frustration, calling the car 'horrible,' to which Kenny adds, 'What's the point of a car if you can't drive with it?' Kenny then laments, 'And this is before the challenge has even started. Walk to start the walk,' while Sharky lightheartedly compares himself to 'Mo Farah's cousin.' After a 25-minute hike, accompanied by tense, adventurous background music, they reach a scenic overlook with expansive views of rocky cliffs and dense forest. The narrator announces that 'Team Winner Champions' (referring to Team Beta) have reached their first location and it's time for them to hear from AJ. Kenny and Sharky check a smartphone, where AJ SHAHEEL appears in a stylized, pixelated video feed with a water droplet icon. Kenny refers to AJ as 'that prick AJ' who has a video message. AJ, dressed in a black jacket and cap, delivers a survival-themed 'CHALLENGE ONE' message in a serious, commanding tone, welcoming them to the Outback. He emphasizes water scarcity, stating, 'One of the most important things here to survive is drinkable water, which you guys don't have,' and warns that in these conditions, the time one can survive without water 'drops drastically.' AJ then reveals he has some water, leading to the first challenge: 'All you have to do is find me.' He promises a reward for finding him that puts them ahead, but warns of consequences 'if you come last.' Kenny, confused, interjects, 'He didn't even say that in English.' The environment shifts to a sunlit, open hillside with warm lighting, dry grass, and red soil, reinforcing the challenging wilderness setting.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with AJ SHAHEEL, the Games Master, appearing in a stylized, pixelated retro-game feed, his voice echoing as he delivers a survival challenge: "All you have to do is finish this rhyme. In the Outback, there are two nutsacks. And all you have to do is check your..." before a chorus of voices emphatically finishes, "Rucksacks! Rucksacks! Rucksacks! Rucksack!" The scene transitions to an aerial view of a rugged, forested Australian Outback landscape, emphasizing the remote setting. The focus shifts to Team Beta—Kenny and Sharky—dressed in beige outdoor gear, walking through the dry grassland. Kenny wears a black beanie, while Sharky sports a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat. They are seen reacting to a smartphone, with Sharky exclaiming, "What was we hang on. In the Outback, there are two nutsacks. There's loads of nutsacks, there's two, four, five. Yeah, good poetry." Kenny adds, "He's got one," followed by Sharky's playful, "You got one. Oh, you got a single one," accompanied by laughter. They then crouch to inspect their rucksacks, with sounds of rustling as Kenny pulls out a compass, exclaiming, "Oh, I've got a compass!" Sharky unfolds a map, which is shown in a pixelated, game-style overlay labeled 'JOURNEY 1' with 'MOUNT AIRLY CAMPING AREA' and a red 'X' marking the destination. Kenny states, "A map. Compass. I reckon map. Let's see this," as the map rustles open. They debate the route, with Kenny stating, "Oh, so this is where we are," and Sharky pointing out, "It says we're here and AJ's all the way over here." Tension builds as they try to reconcile the map's instructions with their current location, Sharky noting, "But it says one hour 20 minutes." Kenny, expressing confusion, mutters, "Walk. I think so. He's lost his f***ing mind," against the backdrop of the bright, sunlit Outback with dry grass, scattered trees, and rocky hills. Sharky then suggests a strategy, "AJ's there. Do you know what would be an L if we go that way? And the whole time it was that. Should we live life on the edge and rock paper scissors?"

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Team Beta, Kenny (in a black beanie and beige gear) and Sharky (in a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat and matching outfit), standing in the Australian Outback. After playing 'rock, paper, scissors,' one of them remarks, "That was a very expensive game we just played." Sharky holds a 'MAP 2 CHALLENGE' and gestures excitedly as they discuss their task: "We start here, and we've got to go down the hill and reach AJ." They express frustration about descending the "hill we just walked up" for 20 minutes, questioning the map's 'COMPASS USELESS' warning with, "What's the compass for then? There's no, there's not even north, east, east, south, west." One of them jokingly concludes, "AJ just wants us to die in the Outback." Kenny checks his phone, displaying a "2 hours 46" countdown, while another voice admits, "I don't know how to follow this map." An aerial view showcases the rugged, forested terrain, emphasizing their isolation. A dramatic narrator's voice then states, "It seems the boys aren't happy. It seems they've been set up by that pesky little man. Let's see what he has to say for himself." The scene cuts to AJ SHAHEEL, 'THE GAMES MASTER,' driving an SUV, speaking with a serious tone through a stylized challenge interface. He confirms, "All right, I'm in the car. It's time to head to my location," and speculates on the teams' struggles: "I think both teams are stranded. They have no water, nothing. I've only seen a glimpse of Nico and Chance's journey. I can only imagine..." Dynamic text overlays and animated graphics, including a pixelated 'JOURNEY 1' map and a water droplet icon, enhance the survival-game atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a man in a black cap and suit driving an SUV along a dusty road, setting an adventurous tone, followed by a sweeping aerial view of a lush, mountainous jungle establishing the remote Australian Outback. A commentator's voice asks, "What Sharky and Kenny up to?" as the scene shifts to Team Beta, Kenny and Sharky, in beige outdoor gear, sitting on a log. Kenny, wearing a black beanie, expresses concern, stating, "I'm worried, we're just walking aimlessly," and suggests, "Let's just have a plan, let's sit down." Sharky, in a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat, holds a map, remarking that the compass "should help us, but neither of us know how to read it," to which Kenny interjects, "And the map." They discuss their route, with Sharky initially confused about going "Southeast. So we're going to go south," before Kenny clarifies, "Southeast. You're all right." Sharky then realizes his error, saying, "I was going to make us go all the way up there," with Kenny agreeing, "Exactly, bro. I went by the rocks." Sharky exclaims, "Oh my god," as Kenny firmly reiterates, "Southeast is where we need to go." Their conversation continues with Sharky still a bit unsure about north, and Kenny making a seemingly unrelated comment about someone living in America, which Sharky responds to with "Good one. Okay." On-screen text indicates they are heading south, and a stylized animated map labeled 'JOURNEY 1' shows their location near 'Mount Airly Camping Area' and their destination. The men interact with the map, pointing and discussing directions, with Sharky excitedly confirming their path. A close-up reveals the map's compass and route details. As Kenny, backpack on, walks ahead through the forest with Sharky following, rustling sounds accompany their movement. A commentator praises their "very good acting" and speculates they "turned off the cameras and used their phones." The commentary then shifts to another team, who are heard discovering "how echoes work," followed by a loud, drawn-out, echoing "AHHHH!" and a distant "Hello!" The final frames show Kenny and Sharky walking through the forest, calling out 'HELLO' as they explore, with the environment shifting from dense trees to rocky, open terrain under a bright sky, emphasizing the isolation and adventure of the Outback.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with AJ SHAHEEL, dressed in a black jacket and cap, standing in the Australian Outback, urgently shouting into a blue and silver megaphone. His amplified voice, sounding slightly panicked, warns, "I'm waiting for you guys. I'm here in the middle of the Outback, I'm kinda scared. There's wild dogs here. There could be a croc in here." He then makes a distinct "croc" sound before clarifying, "That's an edit. Get set." The scene cuts to Team Beta—Kenny and Sharky—walking along a gravel road in beige outdoor gear with backpacks, reacting with surprise and alertness to AJ's voice. Kenny exclaims, "Oh no no no no no," while Sharky comments, "He's on the pretty mockery now," then adds, "Yeah, no, we just heard some noises come from out there, so we thought we'd respond, but I just think it's an animal." A pixelated map labeled 'JOURNEY 1' appears, showing the route to 'MOUNT AIRLY CAMPING AREA' with a red 'X' marking the destination, and inset images of Chunkz and Niko from Team Winner-Champions. AJ continues shouting into the megaphone, his voice echoing as he calls out, "Chunkz? Can you hear me? Niko?" Kenny looks around, confused and concerned, asking, "Hello?" AJ moves along the edge of a water body, shouting "Sharky!" Sharky responds with a loud "Yo!" AJ then calls out, "Anyone? Is anyone here?" as Kenny and Sharky look around with growing tension, one of them calling back, "AJ! AJ!" One of them says, "Wait. I heard his voice," followed by repeated calls of "AJ! AJ!" Sharky then exclaims, "I heard someone, I heard someone. I heard that hey Sharky. Oh my god." AJ's amplified voice asks, "Sharky, can I hear your voice?" prompting more urgent calls of "AJ! AJ!" from the duo. AJ shouts "Yes!" confirming he heard them. Sharky excitedly says, "Oh, I can hear him, I can hear him, I can hear him," then adds, "Alright, now that someone's coming, I'm gonna scare him." The atmosphere is tense and survival-focused, with natural lighting casting long shadows, emphasizing the remote wilderness setting, as a distinct dog barking sound is heard. One of them exclaims, "What? Is he beefing a gorilla? Maybe he's in the woods, we gotta go in," followed by a panicked "Oh! Oh!"

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of the Australian Outback, featuring dry grasslands and scattered trees under an overcast sky, accompanied by adventurous background music. Team Beta, Kenny (in a black beanie) and Sharky (in a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat), walk through the field, with Sharky holding a smartphone and commenting on the environment, noting he's only seen kangaroos on TV, while an unidentified voice denies something. A narrator's voice humorously describes kangaroos as native to the Outback, ready for a fight, but these ones are hopping away from 'Nico's stink breath.' Suddenly, Sharky excitedly shouts, 'Oh my gosh, look at them! Look at them!' as he spots a Wild Kangaroo hopping across the field, prompting both to run toward it, with Sharky continuing to describe its movement: 'Jumping off. There's one big one there. You see that? One big one.' Kenny, amazed, exclaims, 'I can't believe it! Guys, we have just seen real-life kangaroos in Australia!' before Sharky expresses disappointment as the kangaroo moves away, saying, 'No, he's going, mate. He's gone, he's absolutely gone.' The scene cuts to a Pixelated Map Interface labeled 'JOURNEY 1,' showing their path and destination with inset images of Chunkz and Niko, as the narrator announces it's not long until the boys see AJ. Kenny, checking his phone, spots AJ and exclaims, 'Oh, there he is! Look! There's H!' then jokingly remarks on AJ's size, 'He's a short bastard. He's so small, I'm surprised we can see him from here.' AJ SHAHEEL, standing by the field's edge, urgently shouts into a megaphone, 'Guys, where you are? Shut up! Shut up!' while on-screen text confirms 'CHALLENGE 1 COMPLETE' for 'TEAM WINNERCHAMPIONS.' Kenny, frustrated, complains, 'We're filming. Two and a half hours walking,' and directly tells AJ, 'I genuinely despise you for this, AJ.' AJ, with a calm, slightly smug tone amplified by the megaphone, taunts Team Beta, 'Well, congratulations. At least you guys came before these losers,' adding, 'You guys came in second. They came here literally ten minutes ago,' to which Kenny mutters, 'Oh, shut the hell up.' The final shot shows Kenny and Sharky standing together, smiling and preparing for the next leg of their journey, with Sharky holding a smartphone and Kenny adjusting his backpack, as the remote Outback environment remains consistent under the late afternoon or early evening light.

Please tell me we're close. We should be here,” as they approach a wooded area. His mood shifts from fatigue to excitement, believing they've reached their destination, only for a sudden, amplified shout of “AH!” from AJ SHAHEEL, delivered through a blue and silver megaphone, to abruptly interrupt their journey, adding a jolt of urgency.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Team Beta, Kenny and Sharky, standing together in the Australian Outback, both dressed in beige outdoor gear with backpacks. Kenny, sporting a black beanie with sunglasses, and Sharky, wearing a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat, are focused on a smartphone. Kenny exclaims "that's legit, bro" as they react with excitement, confirming they've completed a challenge, with on-screen text reading 'WE GOT IT' and 'LOOK CAN YOU JUST GO BACK TO HIM'. They discuss their next task, with Kenny stating "we got to go to the next point in the map" and both agreeing they need to "find an animal." The mood shifts as Sharky pulls out a water bottle, excitedly showing it off with "look, look at this water they gave me," to which Kenny playfully responds, "Ah, so dumb, man." Suddenly, they both grab water bottles and begin running down a dirt path, laughing and shouting. Sharky yells "Give me the bottle, give me the bottle" as they sprint, with Kenny playfully chasing him, urging "Go holding it." The camera follows their energetic run through the dry, wooded terrain, capturing their continuous laughter and playful banter. As they run, Sharky shouts "George, George, I'm sorry for what I'm about to do, George," and the camera cuts to a close-up of a man with glasses, identified as George, reacting with surprise. The scene ends with Kenny stumbling and dropping his backpack, shouting "WOAH" amidst more laughter and sounds of exertion, as they continue their chaotic, joyful run through the Outback under warm, golden late afternoon light.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute begins with Kenny and Sharky, both in beige outdoor gear, playfully wrestling and laughing on a dirt path in the Australian Outback, their grunts and amusement audible. Kenny, wearing a black beanie, is lifted and spun by Sharky, who sports a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat, as Sharky exclaims, "YOU’RE SICK IN THE HEAD!" Their playful interaction quickly shifts as they stop, exchange a handshake, and begin walking along a narrow, rocky dirt path through dry forest. Kenny voices his thirst, stating, "I’M THIRSTY," and later, "OH MY GOSH WE’RE STUPID," a sentiment echoed by Sharky as they both acknowledge being "so dumb." A male narrator's voice then provides context, explaining that "While Team Beta was much closer to their destination, they were running low on luck, trying to find any animals." As Kenny and Sharky continue walking, discussing their next move, the environment transitions to a roadside area where they encounter a dark SUV parked on the shoulder. Sharky reacts to a strong odor, commenting, "IT STINKS HERE," and questioning, "Why do you think it stinks? That's why it stinks," as the camera pans to show a man in a blue shirt near the vehicle. An unidentified voice from the roadside then wishes them, "Hope you find your way." The weather appears to be worsening, and the narrator's voice returns, stating, "As the weather was getting worse, Sharky and Kenny were taking to desperate measures." The scene ends with Kenny and Sharky walking away from the road, deeper into the rugged terrain under a darkening sky, their focus intensifying as they head toward 'THE TEAM BETA', anticipating they will be found.

segments.integrated_caption: Team Beta, consisting of Kenny and Sharky, dressed in beige outdoor gear with backpacks, walks through a dimly lit outdoor area at dusk, approaching a seated Man in Black Suit and Cap under a covered patio. Kenny waves, while Sharky holds a yellow snack bag. The Man in Black Suit and Cap, acting as a host, greets them, noting, "You guys are ahead right now." Kenny, with a slightly exasperated tone, responds, "We are, but this is not fun what you put us through," and later defends their performance, stating, "I don't think you understand the hike that we just did." The host teases, "You guys should be pumped right now. You guys had the snacks." Kenny, in a playful, sarcastic tone, also makes comments about "AJ," saying, "I see AJ... You're the best guy ever. I missed you," then immediately adds, "Lying through my teeth. I hate him," with Sharky chiming in, "Can't stand his face." As the group sits on a bench-like structure, the host asks, "First team is here. Should we wait for the other team to come?" Kenny or Sharky quickly replies, "Nope," with others agreeing the other team "might take hours." The scene cuts to an aerial view of a winding road through a dense forest, accompanied by a narrator's voice stating, "Speaking of the other team, it looks like they found Nick's retreat. Challenge two." The camera returns to the group, now including a third man in a black suit and cap. The host greets them with "What's up, boys?" and invites them to "Come take a seat," which they do, with Kenny complaining, "Oh my days, this bag's so heavy." The host observes, "All the boys have arrived. You guys been enjoying your time?" to which Kenny replies, "Nah, man." The host then gets serious, saying, "Okay, listen well," and introduces the next task: "Who took the best picture?" Kenny, holding a smartphone, confirms, "We're on the next challenge." The host then begins to announce, "Team Beta's..." as the segment ends, with the atmosphere tense yet playful under the fading light.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens inside a vehicle at night, where Kenny, Sharky, and the Man in Black Suit and Cap react to the announcement of Challenge Two's winner. An announcer's voice declares "Chunkz and Nico!" as the victors, prompting the Man in Black Suit and Cap to celebrate with a high-five, while Kenny and Sharky look on with amusement, accompanied by cheers and excited shouts from the group. The announcer's voice explains that the winning team had a "closer image, more HD, it seemed more like an animal." The Man in Black Suit and Cap, whose voice is now heard, expresses his fear, stating, "Being up against Kenny in a situation like that, I was terrified, very scared." Kenny's voice is also heard, humorously remarking, "He always rigs them." The announcer confirms, "Team Winner Champions presented the better picture, which means they get first picks of ingredients for the next unrevealed challenge." The group then receives a brown paper bag containing food and supplies, including beans. Sharky's voice is heard commenting on the kangaroo photo, saying, "That kangaroo, we're making steak." Another voice suggests, "If you want to, or you can pick the other thing," to which Sharky responds, "What? Beans?" A different voice confirms, "So one team makes beans," followed by the rustling sound of the brown paper bag. The Man in Black Suit and Cap gestures animatedly while speaking, and Sharky holds up a photo of a kangaroo, which he finds unimpressive, prompting laughter from the group. An announcer's voice explains, "Alongside picking food, Nico and Chunkz also gained privileges to the fire pit in front of them, while the other team are given a bin to start a fire with." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with AJ SHAHEEL, dressed in a black suit and cap with a 'Shush' logo, standing on a reddish-brown dirt mound in a barren, open landscape under an overcast sky. He delivers a challenge via a pixelated, game-like interface, stating with urgency that "the temperatures are starting to drop" and teams "need a campfire." Animated graphics of a pixelated campfire and a bed appear beside him as he speaks. He explains that building the campfire "won't be that easy" as they "need to make it from scratch" and use "ingredients" he provided. He clarifies the stakes: "Whoever can make the best campfire and food will have a place to sleep tonight. The team that loses... will be spending a night here in the..." (ominous music begins as he trails off). AJ SHAHEEL concludes, urging them to "make sure you have the best campfire" and for this clue, "try not to think too much," mentioning "people got high scores for entertainment" in a previous series. The scene cuts to Kenny and Sharky, both in beige outdoor gear, standing in a dark, wooded area at night. Kenny, wearing a black beanie, holds a smartphone and reacts with a slightly muffled "Alright, is it?", while Sharky, in a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat, listens intently. Kenny, sounding concerned, asks, "Where are we going to find entertainment in this time?" Sharky, with a clear voice, deduces, "So, basically, we need to entertain AJ," to which Kenny adds, "And cook for him in order to win this." Sharky then expresses frustration, asking, "You think I want to entertain when I'm cold and tired?" The video then cuts to Kenny and Sharky now seated on a log. Kenny tells Sharky, "Listen. Snap out of it." Sharky playfully punches Kenny, echoing, "Yeah, you snap out of it." Kenny reacts dramatically, exclaiming, "Hey, stop! Oh! I was trying to wake you up! I was trying to give you a pep talk! You punched my spine! My spinal cord just got punched!" The atmosphere shifts from serious challenge delivery to lighthearted banter, highlighting the contrast between the harsh environment and the team's camaraderie.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with an aerial view of Nics Retreat, a remote Outback campsite nestled among trees and dirt roads, setting the stage for a survival challenge. A deep, serious narrator's voice explains the challenge is to decide "who was going to have to stay overnight in the Outback." The video then cuts to an indoor, rustic shelter with wooden walls, where AJ SHAHEEL, dressed in a black suit and cap with a 'Shush' logo, sits on a wooden bench. He is surrounded by his team: Kenny, Sharky, and Chunkz. AJ SHAHEEL, with a calm, slightly teasing tone, announces, "Okay, it's time for the results. Only two people will be leaving this Outback," as on-screen text emphasizes 'IT'S TIME FOR THE RESULTS'. He playfully teases his teammates about their cooking efforts, holding a metal pot of rice and beans and sarcastically stating, "I really enjoyed you guys' food. Thank you." Kenny, in a black beanie and beige gear, defensively responds that while it wasn't "the best thing on the planet," they "did the best out of the situation." Sharky, wearing a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat and sunglasses, adds that they only used "what we were given." AJ SHAHEEL presses, asking if they "just served rice and beans," to which Kenny confirms it was "all we had," while AJ suggests they "could have gone and asked Nick." Kenny counters that it was "against the rule." Chunkz, in beige gear and a black beanie, intervenes, saying, "Alright, let's calm down, let's calm down guys, let's calm down," before adding, "I really enjoyed you guys' food, though." Kenny echoes his thanks. AJ SHAHEEL then asks about the food again, and Kenny admits, "Food is a bit mid, wasn't it?" AJ SHAHEEL continues his critique, suggesting the food was "more mid than rice, undercooked rice and beans," which Kenny protests, asking, "Who said it was undercooked?" AJ SHAHEEL retorts, "I can smell it from where I was, yeah." With a dramatic pause, AJ SHAHEEL declares, "I've already come to a conclusion," as on-screen text reads 'I FEEL LIKE WE'VE GOT TO DRAW A CONCLUSION'. He then reveals, "And that conclusion is... I feel like we've come to a draw." Kenny reacts with a surprised "Draw?", Chunkz hums in agreement, and Sharky suggests, "Let's run it back then." Kenny quickly dismisses the idea, saying, "I ain't got time for that," as the group erupts in laughter, highlighting the jovial and relaxed atmosphere of their playful, competitive banter. The dimly lit, cozy rustic shelter, with a colorful mural and a yellow fire extinguisher, provides a warm backdrop to their animated gestures and laughter.

segments.integrated_caption: Inside a dimly lit, rustic cabin with corrugated metal walls, a person attempts to turn off the lights, stating, "I'm gonna turn the lights off at least." However, a persistent light remains, prompting a frustrated, "See this light? Can't even turn it off. So we're gonna have to sleep with this light on the whole night." A narrator then announces, "Fortunately, the end was in sight as slowly, but surely, the sun began to rise." From within a hammock, Sharky, wearing a beige shirt and a green 'AUSTRALIA' hat, groggily confirms, "Sun is coming out," before letting out a tired, "Sharky... Oh sh*t." He then sarcastically asks, "How the hell did you sleep for? I slept like a baby." He continues to comment on the slow dawn, "Just waiting for sun right now. Still waiting on the sun, but it's a lot brighter now." His frustration grows as he pleads, "Come out, sun! We need you, son. Let us go! Where are you, sun?" The narration reiterates, "The sky was getting brighter and brighter until it was time to leave the Outback." Sharky then decides, "Now it's bright, let's just get up." He complains about the cold, sarcastically stating, "Look how I've been this whole night. I've been in 90 degrees in this thing," before letting out a pained groan as he stretches and rises from the hammock. He steps outside into a misty, overcast Australian Outback, visibly shivering as he walks through the foggy, wooded terrain, concluding with a resigned, "Oh, it's time to head back, man."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Sharky, a man in beige outdoor gear, walking through a foggy, misty Australian Outback landscape with bare trees and damp ground, remarking, "Have you ever seen a steak? This is crazy." He approaches a rustic cabin where a fire pit with a metal pot containing raw meat is burning, and a close-up shows rice and beans, with on-screen text 'IS THE FOOD THAT WAS MADE' as he exclaims, "What a journey!" Sharky steps outside into the fog, visibly shivering and adjusting his green 'AUSTRALIA' hat, commenting on "that horror piece of steak." He is soon joined by Kenny, also in beige gear and a black beanie, who declares, "Okay Sharky, we survived the Outback experience," to which Sharky enthusiastically agrees, "We did, we did, we did." They share a joyful, celebratory moment, laughing and embracing, with text overlay 'WE SURVIVED OKAY SHACK EXPERIENCE'. An aerial view shows them walking along a dirt path through the rugged terrain, followed by a scenic shot of them walking toward a sunrise, silhouetted against the bright sky. The scene transitions to a panoramic view of mist-covered mountains and valleys, as a narrator announces, "And so the boys survived this amazing journey. Tune in next week for the finale of the Beta Squad Australian Tour. And for one final send-off, the boys did a meet and greet, and it looks like the fans have something to say. Take it away!" with text 'AND IT LOOKS LIKE THE FANS HAVE SOMETHING TO SAY'. The video then cuts to a large, enthusiastic crowd in Sydney, Australia, cheering and waving phones, as a voice from the stage asks, "Sydney, Australia, what do you guys say?" and the crowd roars back, "Beta Squad!" with text 'PLAY THE TRAILER'. The final sequence shows a man in a blue shirt holding a large map of Australia and New Zealand, followed by a fast-paced montage of action: a person falling from a high structure, a group playing soccer at night, and a man in a black suit celebrating with raised fists, all under the title 'NEXT WEEK ON BETA SQUAD', accompanied by upbeat, dramatic music and a voice exclaiming, "Oh my gosh! So you want us to go in the ocean?"
